payload.answer: Memory principles captured: Brain DB as source of truth; Cognee as rebuildable semantic projection; Slack as strict memory intake agent; Telegram and model-cost questions remain open.
payload.decision: Set Brain DB as source of truth; treat Cognee as rebuildable semantic projection; enforce Slack as strict memory intake agent; leave open questions unresolved for later.

entity_resolution.reason: Only component/system names provided; no authoritative IDs available to merge or normalize.

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB is the source of truth for personal memory.
Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection derived from Brain DB (i.e., not the source of truth).
Slack functions as a strict memory intake agent (i.e., it should ingest memory inputs without acting as a competing source of truth).
Open question: Should Telegram be added later as a memory intake channel/agent?
Open question: Which model is the cheapest while saturating performance?

details: Captured durable principles and preserved two explicit open questions without resolving them.

repair_options: If Slack’s ingestion contract is clarified (e.g., write-only vs. validation steps), update the Slack memory intake principle accordingly.
If Cognee’s derivation method or refresh mechanism is specified, capture those operational details as additional system principles.
When Telegram intake decision is made, replace the open question with a concrete policy card.
